content:
What protocol have you used to connect to the server in each application (imap or pop3)?

  • K9 Mail
  • Thunderbird Android
  • Thunderbird desktop on Mac

imap keeps client(s) and server synchnonised. Deletions performed on a client app are deleted on the server. The deletion will also be applied on any other clients connecting using imap.

Pop3 doesn't support synchronisation. It downloads new emails and stores them locally on the client / device. When you delete an email, only the local app's copy is removed.

It sounds like you have K9 setup to use POP3 and Thunderbird for Android using Imap.

You can check which protocol the mobile apps are using by navigating to ... Settings > account > fetching mail > incoming server

Server Name = Can very for imap and pop3 Port 993 = imap port 995 = pop3
creator: platform34

content:
Q1) Where does Thunderbird for Android save POP3 Mailboxes?

The mailbox data is stored within protected internal storage (app sandbox). It's not accessible unless you have a rooted phone.

Q2) Is there a Backup-Restore Feature for POP3 Mailboxes?

Thunderbird for Android doesn't currently offer any backup or restore mechanism for mailbox data.

You can add your idea(s) on Mozilla connect and receive votes from the community. The TB team take these into consideration when planning feature additions and roadmaps. https://connect.mozilla.org/t5/ideas/idb-p/ideas/label-name/thunderbird%20android
creator: platform34

Q1. Both settings agree with yours.

Q2. "Check Mail" is an Earthlink webmail function to retrieve/sync mail with the Earthlink servers. The Earthlink web client appears to be an imap front end. I have it set for manual updates.

Q3. I use a Trash folder for Deleted mail. Mobile client mail goes to mobile trash. The problem I have is that that action is not picked up by the Earthlink servers when I Sync account or Sync All Accounts on mobile. If I move a message on mobile to a folder, say Inbox.calendar, that action is not picked up, either.

Q4. There are no such settings on the Earthlink web client. They don't have a mobile mail app, it's just for account management.

content:
Q1) The problem is with connection security. The only 3 choices you offer are none, start tls or ssl/tls. If you add a choice of just ssl my roadrunner email will work just fine.

You seem to be using the wrong encryption protocol for the port number given.

Port 587 is the standard SMTP port used with StartTLS, not SSL/TSL. The later uses port 465. Have you tried using StartTLS?

Q2) In reference to the appended h, my guess is that the badly formatted email address was copy/pasted into the recipient To: field. Has the sender retried using a typed in email address?
creator: platform34

content:
Thunderbird for Android doesn't support OAuth with pop3 connections. I suspect your desktop is using POP3 and being imported on TB mobile via QR code.

Imap is the modern and recommended protocol.

To setup Imap

You should setup the gmail account manually, instead of using the QR code. If your still on the first time setup wizard, tap 'get started' and skip step 1 & 2.

  1. Tap the top left menu button to open the side bar.
  2. Tap settings > add account.
  3. Enter your email address
  4. Imap settings will be automatically found & configured
  5. Click the sign-in with google button. Login to the webpage.
  6. Grant TB Mobile permission to access your emails.

To remove an account.

  1. Tap the top left menu button to open the side bar.
  2. Tap settings.
  3. Tap the account name.
  4. Tap the 3 dot menu (top right).
  5. Tap remove account.

To continue using pop3

You will need to setup an app password via gmail's webmail settings page. https://support.google.com/mail/answer/185833?hl=en

Once you have the app password ...

Follow step 1 to 4 above to add the account.

  1. On the configuration found box, click the down arrow.
  2. Click edit configuration.
  3. Change protocol = Pop
  4. Change server = pop.gmail.com
  5. Change port = 995
  6. Change authentication = normal password.
  7. Enter your new app password in the password field.
  8. Click next to complete the account setup.

You can view and edit the configuration of an exiting account by navigating to ...

  • Settings > account name > fetching mail > incoming server
  • Settings > account name > sending mail > outgoing server
creator: platform34
